title: How does the CloudDash Agent work?
content:
The CloudDash Agent is a small daemon process that runs on your servers and containers to collect system-level metrics and application logs. It communicates with our backend via a secure, encrypted HTTPS connection, ensuring that your data remains private and protected during transit.

The agent is designed for high performance and low footprint, typically consuming less than 1% of CPU and 50MB of RAM. It automatically discovers running services and applies pre-configured monitoring templates, reducing the manual effort required to set up observability for new infrastructure.
